source: Mordheim core rulebook, Part 3 - Campaigns & Optional Rules (Hired Swords / Dramatis Personae sections), fetched from https://www.broheim.net/downloads/rules/Mordheim%20-%20Part%203%20-%20Campaigns%20%26%20Optional%20Rules.pdf on 2026-07-03. Owner should cross-check against their own physical rulebook printing/errata. These are CORE RULEBOOK Dramatis Personae (distinct from the Border Town Burning-specific ones in /btb/dramatisPersonae.json). List is NOT exhaustive — the rulebook explicitly mentions further named characters (e.g. Dwarf gold hunters, Merchants' Guild Burgomeisters, Theodor the marksman of Hochland) whose full entries were not present on the fetched pages. TODO: verify vs rulebook p.111-115+ for the remaining characters.

characters.name: Aenur, the Sword of Twilight
characters.mayBeHiredBy: Any warband except Skaven, Undead and the Possessed.
characters.equipment: Ithilmar armour, an Elven cloak, and an enormous sword known as Ienh-Khain (the Hand of Khaine).
characters.specialRules: Invincible Swordsman: always hits opponents on a 2+ in hand-to-hand combat. Wanderer: only stays with a warband for the duration of one battle; a warband that used him last battle cannot seek him out again until they have fought at least one battle without him. Ienh-Khain: allows Aenur to parry, adds +1 to his Strength, and causes a critical hit on a to-wound roll of 5-6.

characters.name: Johann the Knife
characters.mayBeHiredBy: Any warband except Skaven, Undead and the Possessed.
characters.equipment: Countless throwing knives and several long daggers (always counts as having two swords in close combat). Weapons are always coated with Black Lotus; may take Crimson Shade before a battle.
characters.specialRules: Hire fee may instead be paid with one portion of Crimson Shade (he is addicted to it). Knife Fighter Extraordinaire: unlike normal warriors, may combine Knife-Fighter and Quick Shot (throwing up to six knives in one turn if he does not move).

characters.name: Bertha Bestraufrung, High Matriarch of the Sisterhood
characters.mayBeHiredBy: Sisters of Sigmar warbands only. No hire fee — instead, one or more Heroines must search for her after a battle (roll under Initiative), and she will only aid a warband whose enemy has a higher warband rating, per a lookup table (0-49 difference: will not aid; 50-99: 6+; 100-149: 5+; 150-199: 4+; 200+: 3+ on a D6 to agree). A request must be made fresh for each battle.
characters.equipment: Two Sigmarite warhammers, gromril armour, a vial of blessed water, and a holy relic.
characters.specialRules: Knows all six Prayers of Sigmar. High Matriarch: automatically becomes the leader of any warband she joins. Sigmar's Handmaiden: +2 to all rolls to see whether her Prayers of Sigmar are granted.

characters.name: Veskit, High Executioner of Clan Eshin
characters.equipment: Eshin Fighting Claws (extra attack already included in his profile); each claw has a built-in warplock pistol, so Veskit can shoot every turn and fights in close combat at Strength 5 with a -3 armour save modifier (and can still parry twice).
characters.specialRules: Unfeeling: immune to all psychology. No Pain: ignores Knocked Down and Stunned results on the Injury chart — must lose his last wound and be taken out of action to be removed from battle. Unblinking Eye: spots hidden enemies within twice his Initiative value in inches. Metallic Body: grants his high Toughness and a 3+ armour save.
